page: Amazon Web Services m1.large is a first-generation general-purpose instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It is equipped with an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2651 v2 processor running at 1.8 GHz, offering 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The memory configuration consists of 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 840 GB of local HDD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the performance of the m1.large in the bottom 10% of modern cloud servers. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 935.49, while the multi-core score is 1591.77. Memory bandwidth is limited, with uncached large-block reads at 11.1 GB/sec and writes at 9.52 GB/sec. Cryptographic operations benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support, but the CPU lacks modern instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512, and AMX.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m1.large offers a high volume of local HDD storage relative to its compute resources, but its legacy hardware profile delivers low performance density. This instance is best suited for legacy applications, basic file hosting, or low-throughput background tasks that require local storage capacity but do not demand high CPU or memory performance.
description: Amazon Web Services m1.large is a legacy, first-generation general-purpose instance designed for basic workloads. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2651 v2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 1.8 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance includes 840 GB of local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks, including a Geekbench single-core score of 475.0 and multi-core score of 630.0, place this server in the bottom 10% of contemporary cloud offerings. Lacking modern CPU instructions like AVX and AMX, it relies on older architecture but retains hardware AES support. Qualitatively, the m1.large provides high local storage capacity relative to its compute profile, making it suitable for legacy systems, basic file serving, and low-priority background processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m1.large is a legacy general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 840 GB of local HDD storage, suitable for low-throughput legacy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m1.large is a legacy general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 840 GB of local HDD storage.
tagline: A legacy general-purpose instance offering high-capacity local HDD storage for basic, low-throughput background tasks and legacy applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2651 v2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores
8.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB per core
840 GB of local HDD storage for high-capacity data retention
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Xen hypervisor virtualization
Best suited for legacy applications and low-throughput background tasks
